mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, the policy-rate path is easing financial conditions and lowering the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, so S&P 500 returns over the next month should be positive.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above the prior 3-month trend marks accelerating labor-market deterioration, which raises recession probability and should push S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the average triggered return is positive across any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in RSAFS and HOUST indicates collapsing consumer demand and housing activity, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: Reject if joint negative changes in RSAFS and HOUST do not produce materially worse 60-day SPX returns than either series alone, or if the joint signal's Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO turns down while CPIAUCSL continues to rise, the economy is experiencing negative supply pressure and margin compression, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined adverse-supply episodes do not underperform unconditional SPX returns over the next 20 days, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power versus INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: A very low or falling FEDFUNDS rate near the zero lower bound signals aggressive policy easing and liquidity support, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next week through faster deleveraging and lower short-rate pressure.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX does not outperform baseline during FEDFUNDS below 1.0%, or if 5-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of below-threshold observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rising PAYEMS series after a prior decline indicates labor-market stabilization, which reduces recession risk and should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises after at least 2 prior monthly declines
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS rebounds are not followed by above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the average response is negative in any post-shock subsample.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized downturn in GDPC1 and RSAFS signals economy-wide demand weakness that should translate into negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as sales and earnings expectations fall.
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP-and-sales weakness does not predict lower 60-day SPX returns than either signal alone, or if the interaction-driven Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflation regime, falling CPIAUCSL combined with stabilizing INDPRO indicates easing cost pressure without a deep output collapse, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of falling CPIAUCSL and stable INDPRO do not outperform baseline SPX returns over 20 days, or if the signal flips negative when INDPRO is stable.
